You Don't Have to Read Clean Code to Write Decent Code

This post took me close to a year to write — it's a collection of real code smells I've seen in my time as a TA, anonymized and cleaned up. Not a Clean Code book recap; more like "here are common patterns that hurt readability, and how to write them so future-you (or your reviewer) doesn't suffer." Naming, var vs let/const, ES6 destructuring, comments, formatting, ternaries, loops, and not mixing Composition API with Options API.
How to Add ESLint to a Vue Vite Project
This post walks through adding ESLint to a Vue Vite project, using the Airbnb or Standard style guide.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - React Advanced - useRef - (16)
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

Next up is a pretty fun Hook called useRef. What's so special about it? In short, it doesn't trigger a re-render, it hands you back an object with a current property, and it's great for holding values that shouldn't change — or for grabbing a DOM node. Let's take a look.
React Basics: useState, the One Hook You Absolutely Have to Get (7)

State is one of the most important things in React Hooks. How important? In short, the moment you need to change something that shows up on screen, you can't escape React state. In this post I'll walk through useState — starting from Vue's v-model so the Vue folks have something familiar to anchor to — then dig into how React handles data, why it's called one-way binding, and what types you can throw at useState.
You'll Have to Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - React Basics - What Even Is React? - (1)
You'll Have to Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

No matter what, you've got to start by getting comfortable with React's basic concepts and a bit of its background. That groundwork makes it way easier to spot the differences when you pick up other frameworks later. I'm a Vue developer myself, so I'm writing this with the mindset of someone adding one more framework to the toolbox — which means you'll see a few side-by-side comparisons between Vue and React syntax. Mostly I want to make this an easy on-ramp for fellow Vue devs. Enough preamble, let's get into it.
A Quick Trick for Resetting Vue Data to Initial Values
We often need to reset data to its initial values during development. This post is a quick tip for doing exactly that — and the trick works on both Vue 2 and Vue 3.

Vue 2 Two-Way Binding: Concept and Implementation

A quick note on how Vue implements v-model, and what implementing something similar from scratch (without Vue) would look like. Focused on Vue 2 — long-ish post since the focus is on implementation details.
